cards.front: What are the three names for the class being inherited from?
What are the three names for the class doing the inheriting?

cards.text:
Construction order: {{c1::Base}} portion first, then {{c2::Derived}} portion.
Destruction order: {{c3::Derived}} portion first, then {{c4::Base}} portion.

cards.concept: Access specifier changes with inheritance types
cards.explanation:
Public inheritance (most common):
  • Public → Public
  • Protected → Protected
  • Private → Inaccessible
Private inheritance:
  • Public → Private
  • Protected → Private
  • Private → Inaccessible
Protected inheritance:
  • Public → Protected
  • Protected → Protected
  • Private → Inaccessible
examples: class Derived: public Base { }; // Use this

cards.front: What can derived classes do with inherited functionality?
cards.back:
• Add new functions
  • Change how base functions work (override)
  • Change an inherited member's access level
  • Hide functionality

cards.text:
When constructing a derived class:
1. Memory is {{c1::set aside}}
2. {{c2::Derived}} constructor is called
3. {{c3::Base}} object is constructed first
4. Derived {{c4::initializer list}} runs
5. Derived {{c5::constructor body}} executes

cards.front: What are the three access specifiers in C++?
cards.back:
• public (accessible by anybody)
  • private (only by class members and friends)
  • protected (by class, friends, and derived classes)

cards.front: What is the general best practice regarding multiple inheritance?
cards.back: Avoid multiple inheritance unless alternatives lead to more complexity. Most problems can be solved with single inheritance.

cards.text: When a derived class inherits from a base class, the derived class acquires all of the {{c1::members}} of the base class (subject to {{c2::access restrictions}}).

cards.front: What relationship does inheritance model, and what relationship does composition model?
cards.back: Inheritance models an "is-a" relationship (a Dog is-an Animal). Composition models a "has-a" relationship (a Car has-an Engine).
